title: festuca brachyphylla subsp. saximontana
intro: Festuca brachyphylla subsp. saximontana, also known as Rocky Mountain fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This perennial grass has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties. The plant's leaves and stems contain compounds like saponins and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities, contributing to its health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Festuca brachyphylla subsp. saximontana can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote respiratory health and overall well-being.
